qa_pairs.question: What factors should be considered when choosing a revision control tool for a new project?
qa_pairs.answer: When starting a new project, the choice of a revision control tool will depend on the breadth of the project, including the number of contributors and whether external collaborators are involved.

qa_pairs.question: For what type of new project would a first-generation revision control tool like SVN be an appropriate choice?
qa_pairs.answer: A first-generation tool like SVN can be sufficient for a new project with only a few contributors, if it will remain private and primarily requires a history of all modifications.

qa_pairs.question: When is it advisable to use a second-generation revision control tool, such as Git or Mercurial, for a new project?
qa_pairs.answer: For larger projects that will involve external collaborators, it is advisable to consider a second-generation revision control tool like Git or Mercurial.
